auxiliary.previewTaesdDesc: Use lightweight VAE model for fast preview generation
auxiliary.previewTaesdApplicable: Low VRAM scenarios or when fast feedback is needed
auxiliary.previewLatent2rgbDesc: Directly convert latent space to RGB image
auxiliary.previewLatent2rgbAdvantage: Fastest preview method, almost no extra resource usage
auxiliary.previewLatent2rgbDisadvantage: Lower preview quality, for reference only
auxiliary.previewNoneApplicable: For maximum performance or batch generation

network.title: Network & Service Architecture
network.desc: Configure network policies for UI panel and API exposure.
network.listenDesc: Allow other devices in the LAN to access ComfyUI Web interface via IP address
network.listenDefault: When disabled, only accessible via localhost (127.0.0.1), limited to local machine
network.listenEnabled: Can be accessed from other devices in the LAN via local IP (e.g., 192.168.1.100:8188)
network.portDesc: Set the port number for ComfyUI Web service
network.tlsDesc: Enable HTTPS encrypted connection to protect data transfer security
network.lanAccessFunction: Allow other devices in the LAN to access ComfyUI Web interface via IP address
network.lanAccessDefault: When disabled, only accessible via localhost (127.0.0.1), limited to local machine
network.lanAccessAfterEnable: Can be accessed from other devices in the LAN via local IP (e.g., 192.168.1.100:8188)
network.lanAccessSecurityWarning: Any device that can access your LAN can access ComfyUI after enabling
network.webPortFunction: Set the port number for ComfyUI Web service
network.httpsFunction: Enable HTTPS encrypted connection to protect data transfer security
network.corsFunction: Allow web pages from different domains to access ComfyUI API
network.corsTechNoteFull: CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing) is a browser security mechanism that blocks cross-origin requests by default
network.corsDefaultBehavior: When disabled, only same-origin requests allowed (same protocol, domain, port)
network.corsProdSuggestion: Do not enable CORS in production, use reverse proxy (e.g., Nginx) to configure more refined cross-origin policies
network.corsDesc: Allow web pages from different domains to access ComfyUI API
network.corsTechNote: CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing) is a browser security mechanism that blocks cross-origin requests by default
network.securityWarningDesc: Any device that can access your LAN can access ComfyUI after enabling
network.mobileAccess: Access ComfyUI on mobile or tablet
network.teamCollab: Team collaboration, multiple users sharing one ComfyUI instance
network.remoteAccess: Remote access (requires port forwarding or VPN)
network.firewallSuggestion: Recommend using firewall rules to restrict access
network.noPublicWifi: Do not enable on public networks (e.g., cafe WiFi)
network.sslForPublic: For public access, recommend using SSL/TLS encryption
network.port8188Occupied: Port 8188 is occupied by another program
network.multiInstance: Need to run multiple ComfyUI instances simultaneously
network.enterprisePort: Enterprise environment has specific port requirements
network.portChangeHint: After changing port, access ComfyUI with new port (e.g., http://localhost:8189)
network.avoidReservedPorts: Avoid system reserved ports (1-1023) and common service ports (e.g., 80, 443, 3306, 5432)

paths.title: Workspace Directory Mapping
paths.desc: Redirect image input/output and temporary cache storage locations.

performance.title: Performance Settings

cudaMallocHelp.title: CUDA Memory Allocator Settings
cudaMallocHelp.function: Control PyTorch's CUDA memory allocation strategy, affecting VRAM management and fragmentation handling.
cudaMallocHelp.optionAuto: Auto: Let PyTorch automatically choose the best allocator based on environment
cudaMallocHelp.optionEnable: Enable: Use PyTorch's CUDA memory allocator, reduces memory fragmentation, suitable for long-running sessions
cudaMallocHelp.optionDisable: Disable: Use system default allocator, suitable for debugging or resolving specific compatibility issues
cudaMallocHelp.usage1: VRAM fragmentation issues during long-running sessions
cudaMallocHelp.usage2: Memory allocation errors in specific CUDA operations
cudaMallocHelp.tip: Keep Auto for most cases, only adjust when encountering VRAM issues

performance.rtx20Note: Does not support Flash Attention 2 and SageAttention (requires bf16)
performance.xformersStatus: xFormers is a Transformer acceleration library developed by Meta, but is no longer recommended
performance.xformersRecommended: Recommend using more modern attention mechanisms (like Flash Attention) for better performance and active maintenance
performance.xformersAction: Enable "Disable xFormers" option to let ComfyUI use better attention implementations
performance.xformersTip: Recommend disabling xFormers and using system auto-selected attention mechanism for better performance and compatibility
performance.smartMemoryFunction: ComfyUI intelligently schedules models between GPU VRAM and system memory, optimizing VRAM usage.
performance.smartMemoryDefault: Auto-enabled, dynamically unloads/loads models based on VRAM usage, balancing performance and VRAM usage.
performance.smartMemoryEffect1: Models will be more aggressively unloaded to system memory
performance.smartMemoryEffect2: Free more VRAM for other programs
performance.smartMemoryEffect3: May cause frequent CPU-GPU data transfer
performance.smartMemoryUsage3: Need to run other GPU programs simultaneously
performance.smartMemoryWarning: After disabling smart VRAM management, models will transfer more frequently between CPU and GPU, which may cause performance degradation. Recommend disabling only when VRAM is insufficient or encountering stability issues.
performance.smartMemoryWarningTitle: ⚠️ Note: After disabling smart VRAM management, models will transfer more frequently between CPU and GPU, which may cause performance degradation. Recommend disabling only when VRAM is insufficient or encountering stability issues.
performance.smartMemoryTip: In most cases, keep it enabled (don't check this option). If encountering VRAM insufficient errors, try disabling this option, or use with "Low VRAM Mode".
performance.smartMemoryTipTitle: 💡 Suggestion: In most cases, keep it enabled (don't check this option). If encountering VRAM insufficient errors, try disabling this option, or use with "Low VRAM Mode".
performance.channelsLastFunction: Change tensor storage order in memory from NCHW to NHWC format
performance.channelsLastAdvantage: Can improve performance on some GPU architectures, especially Tensor Core operations
performance.channelsLastWhen: Try enabling on RTX 30/40 series NVIDIA cards, may get 5-15% performance improvement
performance.channelsLastTip: Effect varies by model and hardware, recommend testing before deciding to enable
performance.lruCacheFunction: Use LRU (Least Recently Used) algorithm to manage model cache, limiting the number of models kept in memory
performance.lruCacheParam: Set the upper limit of cached models, 0 means disable LRU cache (use default strategy)
performance.lruCacheNote: Setting too large a cache value may cause insufficient memory, recommend configuring reasonably based on available memory and model size

auto.description: Let ComfyUI automatically select the best attention implementation based on hardware
auto.usage: Suitable for users unsure of the best configuration

flash.description: Efficient attention implementation that significantly improves speed and reduces VRAM usage by optimizing memory access patterns.
flash.hardware: Ampere architecture and above (RTX 30/40/50 series), Compute Capability ≥ 8.0
flash.performance: 2-4x faster than standard attention, VRAM from O(N²) to O(N)
flash.usage: First choice for RTX 30/40 series cards
flash.install: Need to install flash-attn whl package
flash.note: Windows users recommend downloading pre-compiled whl file to avoid long compilation time

sage.description: INT8 quantized attention developed by Tsinghua University, plug-and-play, no training needed for inference acceleration.
sage.performance: 3x faster than FlashAttention2, 4.5x faster than xformers
sage.install: Need to install sageattention whl package

split.description: Process attention calculation in chunks, reducing VRAM peak, suitable for insufficient VRAM scenarios.
split.performance: Slightly slower than SDPA, but lower VRAM usage
split.usage: Alternative for 4-8GB VRAM or insufficient VRAM

pytorch.description: PyTorch built-in scaled dot-product attention, optimized for speed, best compatibility.
pytorch.usage: Compatibility priority, old cards (RTX 20 series/GTX 10 series), debugging scenarios

quad.description: Quadratic chunked attention (sub-quadratic), further reduces VRAM usage, slowest speed.
quad.performance: Slowest speed, but lowest VRAM usage
quad.usage: Last resort for extremely low VRAM scenarios (<4GB)

deterministicHelp.performanceImpact: May reduce inference speed by 5-10% due to disabling some non-deterministic optimizations
deterministicHelp.tip: Recommend keeping disabled for daily use, only enable when reproducible results are needed

aggressiveHelp.performanceGain: May get 10-20% speed improvement, but may affect stability
aggressiveHelp.suggestion: Only use when understanding the risks, not recommended for production environments

help.autoFunction: Automatically select based on GPU capability (usually FP16)
help.fp16Function: Force half precision, fast speed, less VRAM
help.bf16Function: Similar to FP16 but wider numerical range, prevents overflow
help.bf16Suggestion: Recommended for RTX 30/40 series, better for some new models
help.fp8e4Function: Essential for Flux models, greatly reduces VRAM with minimal quality loss
help.fp8e5Function: Another FP8 format with larger dynamic range but usually worse than E4M3 for diffusion models
help.fp32Suggestion: Only for debugging or when old GPU doesn't support FP16
help.vaeFp32Function: Most stable decoding, prevents black images
help.vaeFp32Suggestion: Keep this (or default) unless VRAM is extremely tight
help.vaeFp16Function: Faster decoding, less VRAM, but prone to black images/artifacts
help.vaeFp16Suggestion: Only enable when confirmed VAE supports FP16 (use cautiously with SDXL VAE)
help.vaeCpuFunction: Decode images on CPU, saves VRAM
help.vaeCpuSuggestion: "Lifesaver" option when VRAM is insufficient, slightly slower
help.vaeBf16Function: Recommended for RTX 30/40 series, more stable than FP16
help.vaeBf16Suggestion: First choice for RTX 30/40 series, balance of precision and speed
help.textEncFp8Function: Quantization for Flux T5 model, greatly saves VRAM
help.textEncFp8Suggestion: Recommended to enable (especially when running Flux)
help.textEncFp8e5Function: Another FP8 format, larger dynamic range but usually not as good as E4M3FN
help.textEncBf16Function: Recommended for RTX 30/40 series, balance of precision and speed

vram.title: VRAM Strategy
vram.cpuModeDisabled: VRAM strategy is disabled in CPU mode
vram.gpuOnlyConflictWithVram: This option conflicts with the selected VRAM strategy and is disabled when not in auto mode

help.autoModeDesc: Best choice for most users, let system optimize automatically
help.ultraVramConfig: High VRAM mode + Disable memory unload
help.autoDesc: ComfyUI automatically selects the best strategy based on detected VRAM size, no manual configuration needed.
help.autoUsage: Most users, let system optimize automatically
help.normalDesc: Default VRAM management mode, models are unloaded to system memory after use, balancing performance and VRAM usage.
help.highVramDesc: Keep models in GPU VRAM, avoiding unloading to system memory, providing faster generation speed. Note: Text encoders may still be unloaded.
help.highVramNote: Difference from "Disable Memory Unload": This option only keeps main models in VRAM, text encoders may be unloaded; "Disable Memory Unload" forces all content to stay on GPU.
help.lowVramDesc: Process UNet model in chunks, significantly reducing VRAM usage but with notable speed decrease. Frequent CPU-GPU data transfer causes 5-10x slower generation.
help.noVramDesc: Most aggressive VRAM optimization, almost all calculations happen in system memory. This is the last resort when other methods don't work.
help.noVramUsage: For 2-4GB VRAM, or when VRAM is extremely limited
help.gpuOnlyFunction: Keep all content (including text encoders, CLIP models, etc.) on GPU, preventing unloading to system memory.
help.gpuOnlyAdvantage: Fastest overall performance, no waiting for model loading
help.gpuOnlyDisadvantage: Uses most VRAM, may cause out of memory
help.gpuOnlyUsage: For 24GB+ VRAM professional cards, when pursuing maximum performance
help.gpuOnlyWarning: After enabling this option, if VRAM is insufficient, it may cause model loading failure or program crash. Recommended only when VRAM is sufficient (≥24GB).
help.gpuOnlyWarningTitle: ⚠️ Note: After enabling this option, if VRAM is insufficient, it may cause model loading failure or program crash. Recommended only when VRAM is sufficient (≥24GB).
help.gpuOnlySuggestion: For 16-24GB VRAM, use "High VRAM mode"; for 24GB+ VRAM and maximum performance, enable this option.
help.gpuOnlySuggestionTitle: 💡 Suggestion: For 16-24GB VRAM, use "High VRAM mode"; for 24GB+ VRAM and maximum performance, enable this option.
help.highVramBehavior: Main models stay on GPU, text encoders may be unloaded to system memory
help.gpuOnlyBehavior: All content stays on GPU, no unloading at all (more aggressive)
help.reserveVramFunction: Reserve specified amount of VRAM for OS and other software, preventing ComfyUI from using all VRAM and causing system lag.
help.reserveVramUnit: GB (gigabytes), supports decimals, e.g. 1.5 means reserving 1.5GB VRAM.
help.reserveVramMultiTask: Multi-task scenario: 2-3GB (running other GPU programs simultaneously)
help.reserveVramUsage: When running other GPU programs simultaneously or avoiding system lag
help.reserveVramSuggestion: Recommend reserving at least 1.0 GB to ensure smooth system operation. If you frequently experience system lag or other programs cannot use VRAM, consider increasing the reserved value.
help.reserveVramSuggestionTitle: 💡 Suggestion: Recommend reserving at least 1.0 GB to ensure smooth system operation. If you frequently experience system lag or other programs cannot use VRAM, consider increasing the reserved value.

device.title: Device Settings
device.incompatibleDefault: Selected device is incompatible with the current PyTorch backend and cannot be used. Please switch to a compatible device or change the PyTorch version
device.ipexWarning: ipex installed, ComfyUI may prioritize Intel GPU

help.title: Device Help
help.description: Only compatible GPU devices can be selected based on the installed PyTorch version. Incompatible devices are disabled and marked as unavailable.
help.auto: Default option. ComfyUI automatically selects the best available device without manual configuration
help.nvidia: Requires CUDA PyTorch. Supports fp16/bf16, xformers, VRAM management and more
help.amd: Requires ROCm PyTorch. Runs via HIP backend with full fp16, VRAM management support
help.intelArc: Requires XPU PyTorch or CUDA PyTorch + ipex. Runs via oneAPI backend
help.intel: Requires XPU PyTorch or CUDA PyTorch + ipex. Performance is limited, recommended for testing only
help.cpu: No GPU driver required. Available in all environments but extremely slow

vram.applicableScenariosDesc: When running other GPU programs simultaneously or avoiding system lag
vram.config4to6GB: Low VRAM mode + xFormers + CPU text encoder
vram.config8GB: Standard mode + xFormers + CPU text encoder
vram.gpuOnlyWarning: Enabling this option may cause model loading failures or crashes if VRAM is insufficient. Recommended only for GPUs with 24GB+ VRAM.
vram.reserveSuggestion: Recommend reserving at least 1.0 GB to ensure smooth system operation. If you frequently experience system lag or other programs cannot use VRAM, consider increasing the reserved value.

conflict.description: Unmanaged ComfyUI processes exist in the system, which may cause startup failure or resource conflicts. Please choose how to handle these processes.
conflict.portConflictDesc: Detected process using port {port}. Continuing may fail. It is recommended to terminate the conflicting process first.
conflict.killProcessDesc: Terminate all detected processes, then automatically start ComfyUI
conflict.continueStartDesc: Ignore conflicts and try to start ComfyUI (may fail)

versionSwitch.title: Confirm Version Switch

gitError.needCommit: If empty repo, need at least one commit
gitError.useSshOrToken: Use SSH key or access token
gitError.conflictChanges: Conflicting changes between local and remote

gitPermission.aboutDesc: Learn about the causes and solutions for permission issues
gitPermission.addToSafeList: Add problematic repos to Git safe list
gitPermission.ownershipIssue: Git repos may have ownership issues when:
gitPermission.osReinstall: User account changed after OS reinstall
gitPermission.copyFromOther: Copied plugin folder from other computer
gitPermission.adminToUser: Switched from admin to regular user
gitPermission.permissionIssue: When plugin repo has permission issues, you may encounter:
gitPermission.addPluginPath: Add installed plugin paths to safe list
gitPermission.noModify: Will not modify any plugin files or system settings
gitPermission.cannotGetGithubInfo: Cannot get plugin GitHub info (author, stars, etc.)
gitPermission.gitUnsafeRepo: Git operations fail with "unsafe repository" error
gitPermission.gitSecurityDesc: Git 2.35.2 introduced a security mechanism that refuses Git operations when the repository owner differs from the current user to prevent potential security risks.
gitPermission.fixMechanism: The fix adds plugin repository paths to Git's global safe directory list, telling Git these repos are trusted and allowing the current user to access them normally.
gitPermission.officialSolution: This is the officially recommended solution for handling repository ownership changes. You can view or remove these configs anytime by editing ~/.gitconfig.
gitPermission.oneTimeFix: The fix is one-time and permanent. Even if you delete and reinstall plugins, you don't need to fix permissions again.
gitPermission.noteTime: The fix may take a few seconds to minutes, depending on the number of plugins
gitPermission.noteNoClose: Please do not close the window during the fix
gitPermission.noteRefresh: It is recommended to refresh the plugin list after the fix is complete
gitPermission.fixFailed: Fix failed, please check logs for details
gitPermission.batchFixDesc: Batch fix permission issues for all plugin repositories
gitPermission.fixPrincipleDesc: Add plugin repository paths to Git's global safe directory list by running:
gitPermission.fixPrincipleNote: This is the officially recommended solution, safe and reliable. The configuration is permanent, even if you delete and reinstall plugins.
gitPermission.doNotClose: Please do not close the window during the fix process
gitPermission.errorOccurred: An error occurred during the fix process
gitPermission.allFixedDesc: All plugin repository permission issues have been successfully fixed. It is recommended to refresh the plugin list to see the latest status.
gitPermission.partialFixedDesc: Some repositories failed to fix. Please check the failure details for the reason. Successfully fixed repositories can be used normally.
gitPermission.closeWarningDescription: Git permission fix is in progress. Closing may result in incomplete permission fixes for some repositories. It is recommended to wait for the operation to complete. Confirm close?

update.viewHistoryDesc: Select a historical version to switch to
update.cancelAndBackup: Cancel update, backup manually then update
update.closeWarningDescription: Plugin is being updated or version is being switched. Closing may cause plugin corruption. Are you sure?
update.localChangesWarning: The plugin directory contains files you have modified. To protect your changes, the update has been stopped.

updateProgress.closeWarning: Do not close the window during update
updateProgress.closeConfirmDesc: Plugin is being updated. Closing may cause plugin corruption. Are you sure?
updateProgress.depInstallResult: Dependency install results: {total} total, {success} succeeded, {failed} failed
updateProgress.localChangesDetected: Local file conflicts detected with remote updates. You may have modified plugin code or have untracked new files.
updateProgress.forceUpdateHint: If you confirm no local modifications were made, click the button below to force update (will overwrite local changes).

dependency.closeWarningDescription: Dependencies are being installed. Closing may result in incomplete installation. Are you sure?

confirmUpdate.title: Confirm Batch Update
confirmUpdate.description: You are about to update {count} plugins
confirmUpdate.warning: The update process may take a few minutes. Please do not close the window.

batchUpdate.title: Batch Update Plugins
batchUpdate.error: An error occurred during batch update
batchUpdate.partialFailed: {count} plugins failed to update, please check details
batchUpdate.closeWarningDescription: Plugins are being updated. Force closing may cause some plugins to be corrupted or in an inconsistent state. Are you sure?

confirmDisableAuto.title: Confirm Disable Auto Update
confirmDisableAuto.description: After disabling auto update, you need to manually check and update plugins.
confirmDisableAuto.warning: This may cause you to miss important security updates or feature improvements.

workflow.noEnvDesc: An environment is a ComfyUI runtime instance. You can create multiple environments for different projects. Click here to add an environment.
workflow.selectEnvDesc: Please select an environment from the title bar to continue using this feature.
workflow.empty: No workflows yet. Please import or create one.

logging.title: Logging Settings

level.debugDesc: Output all log information including detailed debug info. For development and troubleshooting.
level.infoDesc: Output important business operation info. Recommended default level.
level.warningDesc: Only output warnings and errors. For production environments.
level.errorDesc: Only output errors. For scenarios focusing only on errors.

logging.fileLocationDesc: Log files are saved in the logs folder in the project root directory
logging.autoCleanup: Log files older than 30 days are automatically cleaned up
logging.debugExplanation: DEBUG: Detailed debug info including variable values, function calls, etc.
logging.infoExplanation: INFO: Important business operations like plugin scan complete, environment switch, etc.
logging.warningExplanation: WARNING: Warning messages like missing config, performance issues, etc.
logging.errorExplanation: ERROR: Error messages like exception catches, operation failures, etc.

mirror.enableWarningDesc:
GitHub mirror sources in China are extremely unstable, often experiencing issues like inaccessible servers, certificate errors, and missing repositories.

Only use this as a last resort backup when proxy access is not available.

It is strongly recommended to use a proxy for GitHub access. Mirror acceleration should only be used as an emergency fallback.

about.title: About
about.subtitle: Redefine your ComfyUI experience

projectIntro.title: About Project
projectIntro.description1: ComfyNexus is a next-generation launcher and management terminal designed specifically for ComfyUI. We deeply integrate modern web technologies to provide creators with an ultimate responsive experience, isolated underlying environments, and seamless dependency detection throughout the entire lifecycle.
projectIntro.description2: Here, you only need to focus on the creative flow of nodes and workflows, while ComfyNexus silently handles the complex underlying configurations and update maintenance.

disclaimer.title: Disclaimer
disclaimer.content: ComfyNexus is an open-source/free auxiliary management tool that only provides runtime environment encapsulation and process management. This software does not contain or distribute any copyrighted AI models, weight files, or illegal content. When using this tool to download or run third-party plugins or models, please comply with relevant open-source licenses and local laws and regulations. The team assumes no direct or joint liability for data loss or environment damage caused by the use of this tool or third-party plugins.

about.updateToast: Update check feature coming soon, stay tuned

sponsor.title: Support Project
sponsor.line2: If you find it useful, consider buying the author a coffee

dependency.title: Dependency Management
dependency.description: Python package management and environment configuration
dependency.noEnvDesc: An environment is a ComfyUI runtime instance. You can create multiple environments for different projects. Click here to add an environment.
dependency.selectEnvDesc: Please select an environment from the title bar to continue using this feature.
dependency.autoInstallPytorchNote: Will automatically install PyTorch, torchvision, and torchaudio
dependency.confirmInstallDesc: Are you sure you want to install this package?
dependency.confirmUninstallDesc: Are you sure you want to uninstall this package? This may affect related functionality.
dependency.reinstallAllDepsWarning:
This operation will reinstall all dependencies (ComfyUI core + all plugins), which may cause:

• Dependency version conflicts, causing ComfyUI to fail to start
• Breaking existing stable environment configuration
• Long installation time (may take 10-30 minutes)

⚠️ Only perform this operation when:
• Environment is corrupted, ComfyUI cannot start at all
• Need to thoroughly clean and rebuild the dependency environment
• Following official documentation for environment repair

If only a specific plugin has issues, it's recommended to reinstall that plugin's dependencies separately.
dependency.installCoreDepsDesc: Will install all ComfyUI core dependencies. This is a relatively safe operation, typically used to fix core functionality issues.
dependency.installPluginDepsDesc: Will install all dependencies for the {plugin} plugin.
dependency.analyzingNote: This may take a few seconds

permission.description: Batch fix permission issues for all plugin repositories

operation.2: System will automatically scan all plugin repositories

permission.principleNote: This is Git's officially recommended solution, adding repository path to Git's safe directory list to resolve permission issues.

noticeItem.1: Fix operation only affects local Git config

help.howDesc: The fix adds the repository path to Git's safe directory list, telling Git this directory is trusted.

help.tipContent: If you're unsure about a repository's source, you can check its content before fixing.
